roles.title: Staff Statistics Engineer - Feature Flagging and Experimentation
roles.fit_notes: Exceptional match on core craft—this role is literally building the experimentation/stats infrastructure the candidate specializes in (sequential analysis, CUPED, A/B testing). Staff-level player-coach role with direct product impact and 0-to-1 building on two new products. Datadog is Series D/post-IPO scale with strong builder culture, but lacks AI/ML product focus (lower relevance to growth edge). Compensation trajectory looks realistic at $230–260K for Staff tier at Datadog, though public company equity structure may differ from growth-stage norms.

roles.title: Senior Staff Data Scientist - Bayesian Experimentation & Causal Inference
roles.location: New York, New York, United States; San Francisco, California, United States; Seattle, Washington, United States
roles.fit_notes: Exceptional match on core craft (causal inference, Bayesian methods, experimentation infrastructure) and seniority level—this is a true Staff-level player-coach role with company-wide standard-setting authority. Headway's Series D stage, mission-driven healthcare focus, and emphasis on decision quality align strongly with your values and background. Main gaps: limited AI/ML product development in the posting (healthcare compliance/privacy constraints likely), and compensation is at low end of target ($22-25K appears to be typo; likely $220-250K total based on Series D healthcare companies, which may require negotiation to hit your $230-260K range).
roles.description:
<div class="content-intro"><p>Headway’s mission is a big one – to build a new mental health care system everyone can access. We’ve built technology that helps people find great therapists with the first software-enabled national network of providers accepting insurance.&nbsp;</p>
<p>1 in 4 people in the US have a treatable mental health condition, but the majority of providers don’t accept insurance, making therapy too expensive for most people. Headway is b

roles.title: Senior Data Science Manager, Customer Experience
roles.location: New York, New York, United States; San Francisco, California, United States; Seattle, Washington, United States
roles.fit_notes: Strong fit on stage, domain, and seniority—Headway is Series D growth-stage with meaningful mission-driven work and clear manager-track opportunity. Craft match is solid (experimentation framework, measurement, causal inference all mentioned) with emerging AI/LLM relevance in support automation. Key concern: compensation trajectory is unclear—levels.fyi shows $22-25K (likely typo; probably $220-250K range), which aligns with target, but healthcare software typically lags fintech/adtech on total comp. This role leans more on customer support analytics than 0-to-1 experimentation platform building, so it's a slight step sideways from core expertise, though still high-impact.

roles.title: Director of Data Science - Patient
roles.location: New York, New York, United States; San Francisco, California, United States; Seattle, Washington, United States
roles.fit_notes: Headway is a mission-driven Series D healthcare marketplace with strong product-analytics alignment and a need for senior data leadership—playing directly to the candidate's player-coach strengths and analytics expertise. However, the role is primarily consumer metrics/growth analytics rather than experimentation infrastructure or causal inference at scale, and compensation signals ($220-250K estimated for Director level based on Series D healthcare benchmarks) sit at the lower end of the target range. The 0-to-1 experimentation platform building edge—a core growth driver for this candidate—is less prominent here; this is more about scaling and optimizing a maturing analytics function.

roles.location: New York, New York, United States; San Francisco, California, United States; Seattle, Washington, United States
roles.fit_notes: Excellent craft and seniority alignment—this is a genuine Staff-level incrementality and causal inference role that directly leverages your core expertise (A/B testing, geo experiments, quasi-experimental design). Strong domain fit (healthcare marketplace, not ads/defense/crypto) with clear builder culture and high-stakes decision impact. Moderate concern: Series D growth stage (not 0-to-1), AI/ML is absent from the role scope, and comp data suggests $220-250K range (lower end of target), though Series D healthcare and Bay Area proximity may push higher.

roles.location: New York, New York, United States; San Francisco, California, United States; Seattle, Washington, United States
roles.fit_notes: Strong craft and seniority match: Staff-level product analytics role with explicit focus on experimentation frameworks, measurement strategy, and cross-functional leadership—exactly aligned with candidate's core expertise. Excellent domain fit (mental health, consumer-facing, mission-driven, no ads/crypto/defense concerns). Primary concerns: (1) Series D stage is slightly mature vs. candidate's 0-to-1 preference, though measurement infrastructure may still be greenfield; (2) Limited AI/ML relevance in job posting (growth edge for candidate); (3) Compensation data unclear (levels.fyi shows $22-25K range, likely outdated or incomplete—needs verification against target $230-260K). Cultural signals strong (builder ethos, direct impact, quality-focused). East Coast location favorable.

roles.title: Staff Data  Scientist, AI Products
roles.fit_notes: Strong fit on seniority, AI/ML focus, and domain (productivity tools). Staff-level role on ground floor of new AI division aligns perfectly with player-coach IC preferences and 0-to-1 building appetite. Key concerns: (1) compensation data is incomplete/stale, making realistic assessment of $230-260K target difficult—Dropbox is large-cap, may compress at Staff level; (2) experimentation/A/B testing infrastructure not explicitly mentioned in posting, though likely present in mature org. (3) Maturity of Dropbox vs Series B-D preference—it's a public company, though this is a new division. Strong strategic fit despite size.

roles.title: Staff Data Scientist, Forecasting in Monetization
roles.fit_notes: Exceptional craft and seniority fit—this is a Staff-level role owning critical forecasting systems with direct hands-on work in experimentation, causal inference, and subscription dynamics. Duolingo is a world-class company with proven builder culture and meaningful product impact. However, compensation is a major dealbreaker: the posted range ($204-392K is likely a data error, but even at face value is vastly below candidate's $230-260K target, and Staff roles at public/late-stage companies typically pay below early-stage hypergrowth roles. AI/ML relevance is limited—this is core ML ops/forecasting, not LLM or generative AI work, which is a stated growth edge. Stage fit is moderate: Duolingo is Series D+ (public), not the Series B-D hypergrowth stage candidate prefers.
roles.description:
<p>Our mission at Duolingo is to develop the best education in the world and make it universally available. It’s a big mission, and that’s where you come in!</p>
<p>At Duolingo, you’ll join a team that cares about educating our users, experimenting with big ideas, making fact-based decisions, and finding innovative solutions to complex problems. You’ll have limitless learning opportunities and daily collaborations with world-class minds — while doing work that’s both meaningful

roles.title: Staff Data Scientist, New Subjects
roles.fit_notes: Exceptional craft and seniority alignment—this Staff role is built for someone with deep experimentation/causal inference expertise in a greenfield 0-to-1 setting (New Subjects). Culture, builder ethos, and domain (consumer education) are ideal. Major friction point: compensation data suggests $204-392K range, but salary appears capped well below target ($230-260K), and equity details are missing—critical to clarify before proceeding. Role otherwise checks nearly every box.
roles.description:
<p>Our mission at Duolingo is to develop the best education in the world and make it universally available. It’s a big mission, and that’s where you come in!</p>
<p>At Duolingo, you’ll join a team that cares about educating our users, experimenting with big ideas, making fact-based decisions, and finding innovative solutions to complex problems. You’ll have limitless learning opportunities and daily collaborations with world-class minds — while doing work that’s both meaningful

roles.title: Design Engineer, AI Capability Development (Education Labs)
roles.location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
roles.fit_notes: Strong mission/culture/AI relevance fit, but a meaningful craft mismatch: this role is interaction design + full-stack prototyping focused on education UX and capability development, not experimentation platforms or causal inference—the candidate's core expertise. The seniority level (second technical builder on small team) and stage (mature growth-stage/public-scale company) are reasonable fits, but not ideal. Anthropic's safety-first, principled culture aligns well with values. Compensation likely achievable but unconfirmed. Best case: candidate pivots to AI product design and grows broader product skills; worst case: feels disconnected from foundational measurement/stats work that energizes them.
roles.description:
<div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>About Anthropic</strong></h2>
<p>Anthropic’s mission is to create reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems. We want AI to be safe and beneficial for our users and for society as a whole. Our team is a quickly growing group of committed researchers, engineers, policy experts, and business leaders working together to build beneficial AI systems.</p></div><h2 data-pm-slice="1 1 []"

roles.title: Senior AI Engineer - APM Features
roles.fit_notes: Excellent match on core craft (AI/LLM systems, experimentation, evaluation frameworks) and AI relevance. Datadog's APM team is explicitly in early-stage AI exploration with strong emphasis on correctness, testing, and product impact—aligning perfectly with the candidate's 0-to-1 building mindset and causal inference expertise. Key concern: Datadog is Series D+ (mature growth), not the Series B-D sweet spot; role is senior but may be individual-contributor-heavy rather than full player-coach; EMEA/hybrid location is workable but not ideal vs. the candidate's stated flexibility. Compensation at the high end of Datadog's range ($230-260K) is achievable but not guaranteed given maturity of company.
roles.description:
<p>The APM Features team builds intelligent troubleshooting experiences that help customers quickly understand and resolve performance issues in complex distributed systems. We work at the intersection of observability, product, and AI, transforming large volumes of noisy telemetry data into clear explanations, insights, and actionable conclusions.</p>
<p>This team is in an early, highly exploratory phase of applying LLMs and agentic workflows to real production APM problems. E

roles.title: Staff Data Scientist
roles.fit_notes: Stripe Staff DS role is an exceptional craft match—experimentation, causal inference, and measurement infrastructure are core to the Growth & GTM mission. Seniority and compensation align perfectly ($249-480K range comfortably covers target). However, Stripe is a late-stage scale-up (unicorn/public-adjacent), not early growth stage, and the role is GTM/marketing-focused rather than 0-to-1 product building. AI/ML relevance is modest (personalization/recommendations mentioned but not primary). Strong builder culture and direct business impact mitigate stage concern, but this leans advisory/strategic over hands-on coding.

roles.title: (Associate - Senior) Analytics Engineer, Caper
roles.location: Canada - Remote (ON, AB, BC, or NS Only)
roles.fit_notes: Solid domain fit (consumer retail, not ads/crypto) with relevant analytics engineering work on metrics/experimentation infrastructure, but role is narrower in scope than ideal—this reads as a 'build analytics infrastructure' rather than 'lead 0-to-1 product strategy' position. Compensation is achievable ($248–411K range puts target in lower-mid band), but seniority title (Associate-Senior) and lack of clear player-coach positioning with strategic/product leverage suggest this may be execution-focused rather than IC-to-leadership growth path. Caper (smart carts/retail AI) is interesting and aligns with AI relevance, but posting lacks clarity on how much hands-on vs. strategic/mentoring balance exists.
roles.description:
<div class="content-intro"><p><strong>We're transforming the grocery industry</strong></p>
<p><span class="im">At Instacart, we invite the world to share love through food because we believe everyone should have access to the food they love and more time to enjoy it together. Where others see a simple need for grocery delivery, we see exciting complexity and endless opportunity to serve the varied needs of our community. We work t

roles.title: Analytics Data Engineering Manager, Product
roles.location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
roles.fit_notes: Exceptional strategic fit: Anthropic is a tier-1 AI safety company with clear builder culture, direct product impact, and strong domain alignment (not ads/crypto/defense). The role leverages core expertise in A/B testing, experimentation, and causal inference applied to Claude product analytics at massive scale. Strong growth edge match (AI/ML product via Claude-powered data tools, self-serve analytics innovation). Primary concerns: Manager role is ~70/30 people/hands-on (vs. candidate's 50/50 preference), and comp may be slightly below target at an early-stage AI company, though significant upside through equity.

roles.title: Security Engineer Lead, Corporate Security
roles.location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
roles.fit_notes: Strong organizational fit on seniority, culture, and domain (AI safety org, builder culture, high autonomy), but significant misalignment on core craft expertise. This is a corporate/infrastructure security role focused on endpoints, networks, and SaaS tooling—not experimentation platforms, causal inference, or A/B testing infrastructure where the candidate's deep expertise lives. While the role mentions LLM leverage for security workflows, it's not a growth opportunity in AI/ML product development. The candidate would be trading their differentiated skill set for a lateral move into unfamiliar territory.
roles.description:

roles.title: Research Economist, Economic Research
roles.fit_notes: This is a fundamentally misaligned role despite Anthropic's stellar reputation. The Research Economist position is deeply academic/policy-focused (econometrics, labor markets, structural estimation) rather than the candidate's core strength in experimentation platforms and causal inference applied to product decisions. While AI relevance and company mission align perfectly, the role lacks the hands-on product engineering and player-coach dynamics the candidate seeks, instead focusing on measurement infrastructure and economic index development. The SF-only location also conflicts with the candidate's Philadelphia base and stated location flexibility.
roles.description:

roles.title: Staff Data Analyst, Acquisition Marketing
roles.fit_notes: Excellent craft match—MMM, MTA, causal inference, and experimentation are core to the role, aligning perfectly with candidate's expertise. Staff-level player-coach role with strategic + hands-on split is ideal. However, MongoDB is mature/public (not Series B-D growth stage), Dublin/Ireland location conflicts with Philadelphia base + remote/NYC/SF preference, and marketing analytics is not 0-to-1 product building. AI/ML involvement is minimal. Compensation is solid ($244-289K likely reaches $260K+ all-in), and domain (B2B developer tools) is strong fit.
roles.description:
<p>We are seeking a Staff Analyst for Marketing &amp; Growth Analytics focused on marketing&nbsp; acquisition analysis. In this role, you will serve as a measurement and strategy partner across Marketing Operations, Growth Marketing, and Product—connecting brand advertising, paid acquisition, lifecycle marketing, and in-product growth into a single, ROI-driven measurement framework.</p>
<p>You will lead advanced analytics across Marketing Mix Modeling (MMM), Multi-Touch

roles.title: Senior Staff Data Scientist, Guest & Host Marketplace AI
roles.fit_notes: Exceptional match on core craft (causal inference, user behavior analysis, A/B testing in marketplace context) and seniority level (Senior Staff title, tech lead, player-coach). Compensation trajectory is strong ($241K base+ aligns with $230-260K target). Primary concern: Airbnb is late-stage/mature (post-IPO, $15B+ revenue), not Series B-D growth stage, which conflicts with candidate's strong preference for 0-to-1 building. AI/ML emphasis is present but somewhat secondary to causal inference and personalization—strong but not core to the role.
roles.description: <div class="content-intro"><p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Airbnb was born in 2007 when two hosts welcomed three guests to their San Francisco home, and has since grown to over 5 million hosts who have welcomed over 2 billion guest arrivals in almost every country across the globe. Every day, hosts offer unique stays and experiences that make it possible for guests to connect with communities in a more authentic w

roles.title: Senior Data Scientist
roles.fit_notes: Dropbox offers solid domain fit (consumer/productivity tools) and relevant craft (experimentation, user behavior analytics, AI integration). However, this is a mature, public company—not the Series B-D growth-stage builder environment the candidate targets. The role appears to be individual contributor-focused rather than explicit player-coach leadership, and compensation data is incomplete/unclear, making the $230-260K target assessment uncertain. The AI/Core Experience focus is interesting but doesn't fully offset the stage/seniority mismatch.
